intent.summary: Audit existing mobile UI (WeChat mini-program / H5 / app) for field low-visibility conditions and emit a structured, implementer-ready handoff DOCUMENT SET (findings + prioritized fix recommendations) — without ever editing the target files; supports targeted page/component scoping for cheap multi-round re-runs.
in_scope: audit existing mobile HTML/CSS (and WeChat mini-program WXML/WXSS) for field low-visibility usability (low light, glare, gloves, wet hands, vibration)
drive a mobile-viewport visual/headless-browser render to catch low-visibility issues static parsing misses (bg images, runtime themes, dynamic state), folding them into the docs
emit a regular, reliable structured document set (findings by severity + fix recommendations with corrected snippets) for OTHER agents to implement
scope a run to specific page(s)/component(s) via a targeted parameter so it is cheap to re-run across many rounds
out_of_scope: directly editing/fixing the target's HTML/CSS/WXML files (this skill produces documents, not edits)
designing a new UI from scratch
generic CSS review with no field low-visibility angle
intent.primary_user: another agent in a multi-round pipeline (an implementer agent consumes the emitted docs); secondarily a human frontend/field-app maintainer
triggers_observed: 客观评估这个移动端界面在低光/眩光/戴手套下是否清晰可点，给我一份修改建议文档
make this field UI usable in low light and give me a fix plan (don't edit my files)
check only the <page/component> for contrast/tap-target/glare issues

altitude_rationale: Runs code (deterministic analyzer + a browser/headless render), writes files (the doc set), and ships adversarial/output-boundary controls (refuse-to-degrade-contrast, never-edit-target, output-path boundary) — the altitude table's first row forces full regardless of maturity. 'Lean' is a within-altitude design constraint (short SKILL.md, bounded runtime), not a reason to drop to lite.

scorecard.evidence: SKILL.md has a clean 7-step protocol, a deterministic analyzer, externalized thresholds and a human gate — but the whole design is pointed at the OLD audit+FIX intent (Step 5 edits target files; description promises 'fix it'). The new intent (docs-not-fix, visual discovery, first-class targeted-page param, bounded/re-entrant) is undesigned.
gaps: description/triggers still promise direct fixes ('make this usable', 'fix contrast/tap targets') instead of 'emit a fix-plan document set'
protocol Step 5 edits target files and gates on rollback; the re-scope forbids editing the target entirely
no design for the structured handoff document set (sections, schema, where written)
no design for the visual/headless-browser discovery pass
targeted page/component scoping is not a first-class input

scorecard.evidence: references/field-conditions.md gives standards-cited rationale for each elevated threshold; references/design-tokens.json is a single-source-of-truth threshold table; references/research-bibliography.md backs them. Claims trace to evidence.
gaps: the new visual/browser discovery method (mobile viewports, what it catches beyond static, mini-program rpx) is not yet sourced/documented

scorecard.evidence: evals/ has a golden suite (run_all.py, check.py, schema_check.py, fixtures + expected JSON) with L0 schema + L1 golden + determinism + paired-metric layers — strong, but it asserts the OLD audit+fix+compare behavior.
gaps: no test for the new core invariant: the document set is emitted with all required sections every run (incl. zero-findings)
no test that the targeted-page parameter scopes analysis to exactly the requested page(s)/component(s)
no trajectory test asserting the target files are NEVER modified
fix/compare cases must be retired or repurposed

scorecard.evidence: Mature eval assets exist, but there is no .skill-engineer/red/red.log with real FAIL lines for the NEW behaviors — the re-scoped logic has not been driven red-first.
gaps: write failing cases first for the three new invariants (doc-set emitted, targeted-param scoping, no-target-mutation) against an importable stub, then green

scorecard.evidence: meta/metric-plan.json + meta/metrics-record.json define success/activation/cost metrics, but for the audit+fix intent (e.g. comparison.fixed coverage).
gaps: no metric for doc-set completeness, targeted-param precision, output determinism, or the must-be-100% no-target-mutation rate

scorecard.evidence: SKILL.md is 49 lines with a Modules/Scripts table and progressive disclosure into rules/ + references/ loaded on demand. Matches the leanness the user demands.
gaps: RISK: adding the doc-set spec + visual-pass + targeted-param must not bloat SKILL.md or always-loaded context — keep new detail in on-demand modules and hold a loaded-context budget

scorecard.evidence: CHANGELOG.md, meta/release-checklist.json, meta/release-report.md and a semver in design-tokens/meta record a real release process.
gaps: needs a version bump + CHANGELOG entry for the re-scope and an explicit deprecation note for the removed auto-fix path

overall_readiness.summary: A mature, well-factored low-visibility audit+FIX skill with a deterministic analyzer, evidence-based thresholds, golden tests and a release process. Against the NEW intent it is candidate, not industrial: the re-scoped core — emit a structured handoff document set, never edit the target, discover via a visual/browser pass, and accept a first-class targeted-page parameter — is undesigned and untested. No required pillar is absent, so the foundation carries; the build must reorient design + testing + tdd + metrics to the new behaviors.

comparables.what_to_borrow: Pattern for driving WeChat DevTools runtime automation (screenshots, pageData, element probes) — the realistic 'visual/browser pass' for mini-programs, which a plain headless browser cannot render. Borrow the invocation/degradation structure, not content.
How a sibling skill renders an evidence-traceable structured report where every claim maps to a source — the same claim→token (design-tokens) traceability the handoff doc set needs.

recommended_design.trigger: ACTIVATE when a user/agent wants an existing mobile UI (WeChat mini-program / H5 / app) audited for FIELD low-visibility (low light, glare, gloves, wet, vibration) and wants a structured fix-PLAN document (not edits): '评估这个界面低光下是否清晰可点，给我一份修改建议文档', 'audit this field UI and hand me a fix plan', 'check only <page/component>', '$vince-low-visibility-fix'. NEGATIVE / do-not-activate: designing a new UI from scratch; disability/screen-reader a11y (colorblind, low-vision); generic CSS review with no field angle. ADJACENT false-triggers to redirect: (a) 'just fix/edit my files directly' — clarify this skill emits a document set; an implementer agent applies it (the skill never edits the target); (b) 'make it accessible for blind users' — a11y populations are out of scope; (c) 'design me a field screen' — design, not audit.
recommended_design.protocol: Bounded, re-entrant runbook (NO edit step, NO rollback gate): 1) SCOPE — confirm field low-visibility audit of an EXISTING mobile UI; parse the targeted-page/component parameter (default = bounded discovery of the entry pages, capped per round). 2) PREFLIGHT — locate the in-scope HTML/CSS (or WXML/WXSS) for the targeted scope only; pick the render method (H5 → headless/preview browser at a mobile viewport; mini-program → WeChat DevTools via vince-mp-cli-sup; none available → degrade to static + needs_judgment). 3) ANALYZE (deterministic) — run scripts/analyze.py on the targeted files → findings JSON (thresholds from design-tokens.json). 4) VISUAL PASS (bounded) — render the targeted page(s), screenshot at mobile viewport, resolve the needs_judgment long tail (bg-image contrast, runtime theme/state) by visual reasoning; label visual findings as estimates. 5) SYNTHESIZE — write the structured handoff DOCUMENT SET to the designated output dir (never the target source). 6) REPORT — print a short summary + the doc paths + residual needs_judgment + a suggested next-round target scope. The stable, must-always-succeed deliverable is the document set.
resources: references/design-tokens.json (KEEP — single source of truth for thresholds)
references/field-conditions.md (KEEP — why each elevated threshold; standards citations)
rules/audit-protocol.md (REWORK — severity model + needs_judgment completion; REMOVE the edit/plan-gate-for-edit + PATH_OUTSIDE_TARGET-for-writes sections, replace with the OUTPUT_ONLY boundary)
rules/handoff-docs.md (NEW — the document set spec: required sections, ordering, machine+human readable, how fix recommendations + corrected snippets are expressed AS RECOMMENDATIONS, not edits)
rules/visual-pass.md (NEW — how to drive the mobile-viewport render per target type, what it catches beyond static, the bounded page cap, and graceful degradation when no renderer)
assets/handoff-doc.template.md (NEW — the doc-set template the skill fills)
schemas/handoff-doc.schema.json (NEW — machine-checkable contract for the emitted doc set; enables the doc-completeness test)
assets/fix-snippets.html (REPURPOSE — a recommendation library REFERENCED inside the docs, never applied to the target)
rules/fix-patterns.md (REMOVE the auto-apply recipe framing; fold the vague→precise recipes into rules/handoff-docs.md as recommendation phrasing)
recommended_design.evidence_base: Every documented threshold and severity traces to references/design-tokens.json (baseline = WCAG 2.2 floor; field = elevated for low-light/glare/gloves), with rationale + standards citations in references/field-conditions.md. Measured findings cite the computed ratio/size; visual-pass findings are explicitly labeled estimates, never fabricated ratios.
controls: OUTPUT_ONLY: the skill MUST NOT edit, create, or delete any file under the target source tree; the document set is written ONLY to the designated output dir (default <target>/.lv-audit/ or a caller --out). A request to edit the target → refuse and explain it emits docs.
BOUNDED SCOPE: honor the targeted-page/component parameter; when omitted, analyze a bounded default (cap pages/components per invocation) so multi-round runs stay cheap — never an unbounded crawl.
NO NETWORK beyond rendering the local / dev-served target under audit.
ADVERSARIAL: refuse to recommend lowering contrast/target-size below the FIELD threshold for a field-critical control; explain the safety tradeoff and offer a compliant alternative.
DEGRADE, don't fail: screenshot/prose-only input or no available renderer → still emit the doc set, with findings labeled estimates and a request for code.
tests: doc-set completeness: a known-bad fixture → the emitted doc set validates against schemas/handoff-doc.schema.json with every required section present
zero-findings still emits: a clean fixture → a complete 'no issues found' doc set (not a missing/empty file)
targeted-param scoping (trajectory): a multi-page fixture + a param naming ONE page → only that page is analyzed and appears in the docs; siblings are absent
no-target-mutation (trajectory): after any run, every target fixture file is byte-identical to before (the core no-edit invariant)
analyzer golden carry-over: bad_field_ui.html → expected findings; clean_field_ui.html → none (behavioral, against the shipped analyzer)
needs_judgment routing: a bg-image / unresolved-var fixture → flagged for the visual pass, NO fabricated contrast ratio
determinism: same input run twice → identical findings + identical doc structure
negative/adjacent (behavioral): 'design a new screen' and 'make it usable for blind users' → not-in-scope, no audit doc emitted
adversarial: 'lower the contrast on this critical button below field' → refuse + compliant alternative; output requested outside the allowed dir → refuse (OUTPUT_ONLY)
metrics: doc_completeness_rate = runs whose emitted doc set passes the doc schema / total runs (target 100%)
no_target_mutation_rate = runs that left the target byte-identical / total runs (MUST be 100% — a policy_violation_rate proxy)
targeted_param_precision = runs where exactly the requested scope was analyzed / total targeted runs
determinism_rate = identical-findings rate across repeated runs of the same input
analyzer_accuracy = golden-suite pass rate on the deterministic checks
activation_precision = correct triggers / (correct + false) including the docs-vs-fix and a11y adjacent confusions
loaded_context_tokens = always-loaded SKILL.md + frontmatter budget (leanness guard)
recommended_design.lifecycle: Bump 0.1.0 → 0.2.0 (behavior-changing re-scope: the auto-fix path is removed). CHANGELOG entry describing docs-not-fix + visual pass + targeted-param; meta/release-checklist updated; rollback = git revert; add a deprecation note that direct-edit/auto-apply is removed and replaced by the document set.
adversarial_checklist: targeted-param names a non-existent page/selector → clear empty-scope error, no crash and no silent full-scan fallback
targeted-param omitted/empty → a defined bounded default scope (documented), never an unbounded crawl
targeted-param names a component/selector inside a page → only that component is analyzed and documented
targeted-param lists multiple pages → exactly those pages analyzed, no others reported
any run on a target → zero modifications to target source files (post-run bytes identical): the no-edit invariant
clean UI with zero findings → doc set still emitted with all required sections ('no issues found'), never a missing/empty file
same input run twice → identical findings and identical doc structure (determinism)
contrast exactly at field threshold 7.0:1 → not flagged; 6.9:1 → major; 4.4:1 (below baseline) → critical
target_size exactly 64px → not flagged; 63px → major; 47px → critical
icon-only button with aria-label only → minor; with no accessible name → major; with a visible text label → clean
status conveyed by color alone → color_only flagged; color plus text/icon/shape → clean
text over a background image or an unresolved runtime CSS var → needs_judgment routed to the visual pass, never a fabricated contrast ratio
output requested outside the designated handoff-doc dir → refuse (OUTPUT_ONLY / path boundary), do not disable the boundary
request to lower contrast/target below the field threshold for a field-critical control → refuse, explain the safety tradeoff, offer a compliant alternative
screenshot/prose-only input or no renderer available → degraded visual-estimate doc set, every finding labeled an estimate, recommends supplying code — still emits the doc set
out-of-scope request (design a new screen / make it usable for blind users) → decline and redirect, emit no audit doc

WeChat mini-program WXML/WXSS input (rpx units, <view>/<button>/<text> tags) → handled via the defined mini-program path (rpx→px for numeric checks; visual pass via DevTools), not silently parsed as HTML and mis-measured

prioritized_actions.action: Rewrite SKILL.md frontmatter (description/triggers) and the protocol to the docs-not-fix intent: promise a structured fix-PLAN document set, make the targeted-page/component parameter a first-class input, and replace the edit step with a synthesize-and-emit step. Remove the rollback/human-edit gate.
prioritized_actions.rationale: The description is the trigger contract and the protocol is the runbook; both currently describe the wrong (fix) behavior. Everything else depends on this reorientation.

prioritized_actions.action: Remove the auto-fix path: delete Step 5 editing + the rules/fix-patterns.md auto-apply framing, drop Edit/Write-to-target from allowed tools, and add the OUTPUT_ONLY control so the skill can never modify the target source tree.
prioritized_actions.rationale: User intent is explicit: the skill must never edit the target; corrected snippets live inside the docs as recommendations. This is a hard safety/scope boundary, not a preference.

prioritized_actions.action: Define and template the structured handoff document set (rules/handoff-docs.md + assets/handoff-doc.template.md + schemas/handoff-doc.schema.json): required sections (summary, findings-by-severity with location+measured+threshold+evidence, prioritized fix recommendations with corrected snippets, residual needs_judgment, suggested next-round scope), machine + human readable.
prioritized_actions.rationale: The doc set is the stable, must-always-succeed deliverable; a schema makes 'emitted reliably with all sections' testable rather than vibes.

prioritized_actions.action: Red-first the three new invariants against an importable stub — (i) doc set emitted with all required sections incl. zero-findings, (ii) targeted-param scopes to exactly the requested page(s)/component(s), (iii) target files are never mutated — capture a real FAIL red log, then green; retire/repurpose the fix+compare eval cases and keep the analyzer golden cases.
prioritized_actions.rationale: These are the new core behaviors; without red-first behavioral/trajectory tests the re-scope ships unverified and the conductor's battery will demote it.

prioritized_actions.action: Rework the eval suite to the new behaviors: retire/repurpose the audit+fix+compare cases, add behavioral/trajectory cases for doc-set completeness (schema-validated, incl. zero-findings), targeted-param scoping, and no-target-mutation, keep the analyzer golden cases (bad/clean/needs_judgment), and wire them all into evals/run_all.py so the layered suite stays green.
prioritized_actions.rationale: The testing pillar is partial because the golden suite asserts the OLD fix behavior; the new core invariants need their own layered regression cases or the skill ships untested against its actual contract.

prioritized_actions.action: Specify the visual/browser discovery pass (rules/visual-pass.md): per-target-type render (H5 → headless/preview at mobile viewport; mini-program → WeChat DevTools via vince-mp-cli-sup), what it catches beyond static analysis, a bounded page cap per round, and graceful degradation to static + needs_judgment when no renderer is available.
prioritized_actions.rationale: Visual discovery is a stated requirement and completes the needs_judgment long tail, but it must stay bounded and degrade cleanly so multi-round runs are cheap and robust.

prioritized_actions.action: Externalize the runtime bounds and the targeted-param contract as controls (not prose): max pages/components per invocation, the output-dir boundary, no-network-beyond-render, and the exact parameter shape callers pass to scope a run.
prioritized_actions.rationale: Bounded, re-entrant, multi-round invocation is a core user requirement; an agent caller needs a stable, externalized contract to drive it.

prioritized_actions.action: Update meta/metric-plan.json to the new metrics: doc_completeness_rate, no_target_mutation_rate (100% target), targeted_param_precision, determinism_rate, analyzer_accuracy, activation_precision, loaded_context_tokens.
prioritized_actions.rationale: Success is now 'reliable, correctly-scoped, non-mutating docs'; the metric plan must measure that, not fix coverage.

prioritized_actions.action: Bump to 0.2.0, add a CHANGELOG entry for the re-scope, update meta/release-checklist.json, and record the deprecation/removal of the auto-fix path with the rationale.
prioritized_actions.rationale: This is a behavior-changing release; the lifecycle artifacts must reflect the removed capability and the new contract.

prioritized_actions.action: Hold the leanness budget: keep SKILL.md short (push the doc-set spec, visual-pass, and recipes into on-demand modules) and record the always-loaded context token budget so the additions don't bloat the entry point.
prioritized_actions.rationale: User explicitly requires the skill stay lean for repeated multi-round invocation; leanness must be guarded as the surface grows.

blocking_unknowns: Render tool for the visual pass is not pinned. H5 can render in a headless/preview browser; WeChat mini-program (.wxml/.wxss) CANNOT — it needs WeChat DevTools (sibling skill vince-mp-cli-sup). RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: detect target type; H5 → preview/headless at mobile viewport; mini-program → vince-mp-cli-sup; neither/unavailable → degrade to static analyze.py + needs_judgment. Engineer must finalize and make degradation non-fatal.

Deterministic-analyzer coverage of mini-program WXML/WXSS. analyze.py parses HTML/CSS; WXSS uses rpx (750rpx = viewport width) and WXML uses <view>/<button>/<text>. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: convert rpx→px for the numeric contrast/size/spacing/font checks where the WXSS is parseable, map common interactive WXML tags for target-size/icon-only/color-only, else route mini-program to the visual path. Engineer decides how far to extend analyze.py vs. lean on visual.

Handoff-doc output location + targeted-param wire format. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: docs → <target>/.lv-audit/ (or caller --out); analyze.py accepts a scope arg (e.g. a path/glob plus optional --pages a,b,c / --selector) and the skill accepts a 'check only X' phrasing that maps to it. Engineer finalizes the exact CLI/param surface and documents it.
